Analysis Blocks: Keep only tensions that visibly change player behavior; decorative mappings are noise.
Failure Patterns and Fixes: If one currency bypasses the web, add a cost, dependency, or brake before tuning anything else.

Overview: Frame the system as a visible tension web with costly recovery, upkeep drag, overflow risk, stockpile pressure, and hoarding traps, not as a long list of resources.
Analysis Blocks: Cut repeated map framing and keep only the pressure relationship the player can read and act on.
Reduce the model to a few strong tensions, stockpiles, warehousing, and upkeep drag; only include a variable if it changes player behavior.
Prefer stockpiles, warehousing, spoilage, and upkeep drag over anonymous penalty meters.
Output Format: Prefer a short field set that forces variable role, key tension, dominant risk, and recovery cost.

Quality Checks: Split the checks into visible pressure, costly recovery, dominant-currency guard, and fantasy fit so the loop does not slide back into a generic template.
Use extra non-generic headings only when they tighten pressure readability and compactness at the same time.

Failure Patterns and Fixes: Keep cheap recovery and hidden pressure as first-class failures, then repair readability and cost before adding new variables.

Output Format: End with fields that reveal the key currencies, stockpile traps, main tension, dominant failure mode, and the cost of recovery.
